channel.title: Welcome to my site. on James Akl
channel.description: Recent content in Welcome to my site. on James Akl

item.title: Helpful Resources
item.description: This page is under construction. Details, linked, and content will be updated. I update this page over time with resources and references. These may be helpful to other practitioners, researchers, or students. Lists & Dictionaries List of algorithms List of data structures Outline of algebraic structures Outline of machines Outline of robotics Outline of automation Dictionary of Algorithms & Data Structures [Work in progress.

item.title: Robot Control Simulations
item.description: GitHub Source files are available in the GitHub repository. This page summarizes the main ideas, detailed treatments are in the PDFs linked under the section titles. Three different control methods have been formulated and simulated. State Observer & Feedback Control PDF A linear state observer is designed, specifically a Luenberger observer of the form: $$ \begin{dcases} \dot{\hat{\mathrm{x}}}=\mathrm{A} \hat{\mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{B} \mathbf{u}+\mathrm{L}(\mathbf{y}-\hat{\mathbf{y}}) \\ \hat{\mathbf{y}}=\mathrm{Cx} \end{dcases} $$ The observer gain matrix $\mathrm{L}$ is to be designed.

item.title: Convex Analysis: Select Works
item.description: These interesting problems are handpicked from several of my old projects in Convex Analysis & Optimization and Real Analysis. The solutions and proofs are my own and are linked as PDFs under the section titles. Semicontinuity PDF Let $f : \R^n \to \R$ be a lower semicontinuous function, and $g : \R \to \R$ be a lower semicontinuous and nondecreasing function. Show that the function $h$ defined by $h(x) = g \circ f(x)$ is lower semicontinuous.

item.title: Numerical Analysis: Wave PDE
item.description: GitHub Source code is available in the GitHub repository. Description The project’s full title is: Solving a Time–Dependent 1D Wave PDE A Numerical Linear Algebraic Approach Problem Statement The governing PDE often used to model wave phenomena is the Wave equation: $$ \ddot{u} = c^2 \nabla^2 u $$ Our setting is one-dimensional $(\nabla = \frac{\partial}{\partial x} \widehat{\mathbf{i}})$ and we constrain $x \in [0,1]$. Thus, this PDE is equivalent to,

item.title: FEM Optimization for 1D PDE
item.description: PDF | GitHub Full treatment is in the linked PDF and GitHub repo. This page summarizes the main ideas. Description The project’s full title is: Finite Element Analysis of a 1D Convection–Diffusion Problem Formulation, Implementation, and Optimization Problem Statement Suppose that an ionic fluid is trapped between two flat parallel electrodes. The fluid cations are propelled by the cathode and attracted by the anode. The objective is to determine the cation concentration $C$ at any point in space between the two electrodes.

item.title: Reading Spatial Transforms
item.description: This article covers different ways to interpret homogeneous spatial transforms and provides appropriate notational practices for each. Homogeneous Matrices In the context of robot kinematics, a common modeling choice for forward kinematics is the use of homogeneous matrices. Recall the familiar homogeneous transformation matrix $\mathrm{T}$ as used in forward kinematics, $$ \mathrm{T} = \begin{bmatrix} \mathrm{R} & \mathbf{d} \\ \mathbf{0}& 1 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} r_{xx'} & r_{xy'} & r_{xz'} & d_x \\ r_{yx'} & r_{yy'} & r_{yz'} & d_y \\ r_{zx'} & r_{zy'} & r_{zz'} & d_z \\ 0 & 0 & 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix} $$

item.title: CAD Model: MicroGuard Design
item.description: Renders | Drawings | Materials | GitHub Source files can be found in the GitHub repo. Description The project’s full title is: MicroGuard System: 3D Concept Model Examining Security via Intimidation This project is only a 3D visual concept model, not a fully-engineered system. It's "just like in the movies", only artful visuals and not working systems. The aim is artistic and artisanal, not functional. It is "

item.title: Inverse Problems for DOBC
item.description: PDF | GitHub Full treatment is in the linked PDF and GitHub repo. This page summarizes the main ideas. Description The project’s full title is: Noise Extraction in Disturbance Observer-Based Control A Computational Inverse Problems Approach In the context of control theory, a system with inputs $u$ and outputs $y$ may be subject to disturbances on the input, either as unknown external inputs or as model mismatch (inaccurate knowledge of the system).

item.title: A Tour of Robot Mechanics
item.description: This article provides a conceptual but rigorous understanding of common robot kinematics and dynamics, presented in a forward and inverse problems setting. After careful reading, the ‘whys’ should become apparent, allowing the ‘hows’ to be better appreciated. Motivation The basic functionality we’d like robots to have, is to move. However, describing a motion from point $\mathsf{A}$ to point $\mathsf{B}$ precisely is not straightforward. Consider these two points $\mathsf{A}$ and $\mathsf{B}$.

item.title: Defining a Robot
item.description: In this article we will attempt to define a robot by examining the underlying conditions that distinguish robotic systems. It is impossible to satisfy all opinions with one definition, but being objectively right is not the goal. Instead, the aim is to better understand the nuances surrounding robots. History The word robot has literary origins (specifically, Czech) meaning forced labor, vaguely implying that a robot is a machine-slave. However, a more suitable metaphor for our modern times is that of a “helper”; specifically, that a robot is a machine-helper.

item.title: About
item.description: I am a roboticist with a background in engineering, mathematics, and science. Currently, I am a Doctoral Researcher at Worcester Polytechnic Institute working in the Manipulation & Environmental Robotics Lab. Interests My main interests are in Autonomous & Intelligent Systems. For this, I focus on the relevant advances, sciences, and practices within: Robotics & Automation Artificial Intelligence Control & Dynamical Systems Mathematics & Formal Systems Software & Computing Systems Mechatronic Systems I also find biologically-driven fields (e.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Some of my personal projects are showcased below. [View tags] Convex Analysis & Optimization: Select Works Collection of interesting problems with my proofs and solutions. Numerical Analysis & Implementation: 1D PDE [GitHub] Numerical linear algebraic solution of a time–dependent wave PDE. Finite Element Analysis & Implementation: 1D PDE [PDF][GitHub] Designing and optimizing a finite element solution to a 1D PDE. Inverse Problems for Disturbance Observer Control [PDF][GitHub] Regularization and optimization applied to DOB control.

item.title: Research
item.description: Some of my research contributions are disclosed below. Publications [PDF][Dataset] ZeroWaste Dataset: Towards Deformable Object Segmentation in Extreme Clutter, 2022 IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR). Dina Bashkirova; Mohamed Abdelfattah; Ziliang Zhu; James Akl; Fadi Alladkani; Ping Hu; Vitaly Ablavsky; Berk Calli; Sarah Adel Bargal; Kate Saenko. [PDF][Video] Towards Robotic Metal Scrap Cutting: A Novel Workflow and Pipeline for Cutting Path Generation, 2021 IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE).

item.title: Resume
item.description: My professional and educational backgrounds are shared below. View my resume View my coursework I value openness and transparency, but also confidentiality. For private information, please request directly at james-akl@outlook.com.

item.title: Writings
item.description: Some of my personal writings are listed below. [View tags] Reading Spatial Transforms Different ways to interpret a homogeneous (rigid-body) transform. A Tour of Robot Mechanics Broad but rigorous glide over robot kinematics and dynamics. Defining a Robot An evolving attempt to answer “What is a robot?” in a prescriptive manner.
